---
title: "sym2vec"
generated: true
description: "Vec of the upper triangle of a symmetric matrix block."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.linalg.matrix`](../nitrix-linalg-matrix.qmd)

*decorated:* `@partial(jax.custom_vjp, nondiff_argnums=(1,))`

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:sym2vec -->
```python
sym2vec(sym: Num[Array, '... i i'], offset: int = 1) -> Num[Array, '... n']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:sym2vec -->
Vec of the upper triangle of a symmetric matrix block.

Output entries follow the row-major order of the upper triangle
starting at the diagonal offset by `offset`.  This is the inverse
of [`vec2sym`](../nitrix-linalg-matrix/vec2sym.qmd).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:sym2vec -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `sym` | `Num` | `...` × `i` × `i` | — | Symmetric matrix block. (Symmetry is assumed, not checked.) |
| `offset` | `int` | — | `1` | Diagonal offset. Default `1` (skip the main diagonal, producing $\binom{i}{2} = i (i - 1) / 2$ entries). Use `offset=0` to include the main diagonal. |

**Returns** `Num` — shape `...` × `n`. The vectorised upper triangle, carrying the leading batch axes of `sym` and a trailing axis of length `n` equal to the number of selected entries.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:sym2vec -->
::: {.callout-note}
The hand-rolled `custom_vjp` reflects that this operation strictly
drops half the matrix, so its backward is masked to the upper
triangle: the gradient through the dropped lower triangle is zero
rather than mirrored.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:sym2vec -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `i` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
